label: Clinical Functions
description: Canonical taxonomy of the clinical purpose served by an evidence statement or executable object.
usageNotes: One evidence statement should carry one primary clinical function.
Secondary functions can be modeled later as relationships, not duplicate primary labels.

entries.label: Screening
entries.description: Identify patients at risk or likely to have latent disease before overt presentation.
usageNotes: Use for asymptomatic or surveillance-entry populations.

entries.label: Diagnosis
entries.description: Confirm, exclude or refine the presence of disease or syndrome.
usageNotes: Primary home for diagnostic-test and imaging-test evidence.

entries.label: Severity Stratification
entries.description: Classify disease severity, burden or stage.
usageNotes: Separate from future risk prediction.

entries.label: Risk Stratification
entries.description: Estimate probability of future adverse outcomes or benefit/harm tradeoffs.
usageNotes: Use for prognostic scores, models and risk thresholds.

entries.label: Eligibility
entries.description: Determine whether a patient qualifies for a treatment, pathway or procedure.
usageNotes: Typical for AUC and protocol inclusion logic.

entries.label: Intervention Selection
entries.description: Choose among competing interventions or treatment strategies.
usageNotes: Primary function for surgery vs device vs drug selection rules.

entries.label: Perioperative Optimization
entries.description: Optimize patient condition, preparation and workflow around an operative episode.
usageNotes: Includes ERAS preop readiness and multidisciplinary preparation.

entries.label: Intraoperative Management
entries.description: Guide decisions made during a procedure or operation.
usageNotes: Includes anesthesia, device deployment and operative rescue strategies.

entries.label: Postoperative Management
entries.description: Guide immediate and early post-procedure care.
usageNotes: Use for analgesia, nutrition, mobilization and routine postoperative care.

entries.label: Critical Care Management
entries.description: Direct ICU-level management for unstable or high-acuity patients.
usageNotes: Distinct from routine postoperative ward management.

entries.label: Rehabilitation
entries.description: Support functional recovery, conditioning or return to baseline activity.
usageNotes: Use for rehab strategy or structured recovery programs.

entries.label: Follow-up
entries.description: Guide scheduled clinical reassessment after index care.
usageNotes: Use when the main goal is interval monitoring rather than recurrence detection alone.

entries.label: Complication Management
entries.description: Detect, classify and manage adverse events or complications.
usageNotes: Use for rescue bundles, escalation rules and reintervention triggers.

entries.label: Surveillance
entries.description: Ongoing surveillance for recurrence, deterioration or delayed complications.
usageNotes: Distinct from generic follow-up because the objective is event detection.

entries.label: Escalation
entries.description: Increase intensity of care or move to higher-acuity interventions.
usageNotes: Typically coupled to thresholds or failure criteria.

entries.label: De-escalation
entries.description: Reduce intensity, monitoring or treatment burden safely.
usageNotes: Use for ventilator liberation, antimicrobial narrowing or ICU step-down.

entries.label: Palliative Transition
entries.description: Transition care goals toward palliation or comfort-focused management.
usageNotes: Use only when the evidence explicitly addresses goal-of-care shift.

entries.label: Prevention
entries.description: Prevent primary, perioperative or secondary adverse events.
usageNotes: Includes prophylaxis and preventive bundle recommendations.

entries.label: Discharge Planning
entries.description: Determine readiness, timing and structure of discharge transitions.
usageNotes: Use for discharge milestones, home monitoring and follow-up readiness.

entries.label: Long-term Secondary Prevention
entries.description: Reduce recurrence or long-term adverse outcomes after an index event.
usageNotes: Applies to chronic prevention after surgery, MI, stroke or cancer therapy.
